summary: 科罗拉多州民主党初选中，进步派候选人击败两位资深温和派，包括州总检察长菲尔·韦泽击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特，梅拉特·基罗斯击败连任近30年的众议员戴安娜·德盖特。分析人士认为，这反映了民主党内部代际和意识形态分歧，以及对以色列政策、金钱政治等议题的不满。同时，最高法院维持跨性别运动员禁令，特朗普盟友继续挑战出生公民权。西部野火肆虐，勒布朗·詹姆斯成为自由球员。

vocabulary.en: the group of people in a society or organization who have power and influence and do not want change
vocabulary.excerpt: Establishment Dems Lose Races in Colorado
vocabulary.example_cn: 建制派民主党人在科罗拉多州输掉选举

vocabulary.en: favoring or implementing social reform or new, liberal ideas
vocabulary.excerpt: do Democrats see progressives as a problem?
vocabulary.example_cn: 民主党人是否将进步派视为问题？

vocabulary.en: a person who advocates for a political system combining democratic elections with socialist economic policies
vocabulary.excerpt: Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.
vocabulary.example_cn: 又一位民主社会主义者击败了一名温和派。

vocabulary.en: a person with moderate views, especially in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.
vocabulary.example_cn: 又一位民主社会主义者击败了一名温和派。

vocabulary.en: a preliminary election to appoint delegates to a party conference or to select the candidates for a principal election
vocabulary.excerpt: Stunning losses in Colorado. State attorney general Phil Weiser upsets Senator Michael Bennet in the Democratic primary for governor.
vocabulary.example_cn: 科罗拉多州出现惊人失利。州总检察长菲尔·韦泽在民主党州长初选中击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特。

vocabulary.en: the current holder of an office or position
vocabulary.excerpt: Melat Kiros beat a nearly 30-year incumbent congresswoman, Diana DeGette
vocabulary.example_cn: 梅拉特·基罗斯击败了连任近30年的现任众议员戴安娜·德盖特

vocabulary.en: to defeat an opponent that is expected to win
vocabulary.excerpt: State attorney general Phil Weiser upsets Senator Michael Bennet
vocabulary.example_cn: 州总检察长菲尔·韦泽击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特

vocabulary.en: an expression of sharp disapproval or criticism
vocabulary.excerpt: A rare rebuke from the conservative court. Birthright citizenship upheld.
vocabulary.example_cn: 来自保守派法院的罕见谴责。出生公民权得到维持。

vocabulary.cn: 出生公民权（在美国出生即自动获得公民身份）
vocabulary.en: the right of anyone born in a country to be a citizen of that country
vocabulary.excerpt: Birthright citizenship upheld. How the president wants to get his way anyway.
vocabulary.example_cn: 出生公民权得到维持。总统无论如何都想达成目的。

vocabulary.en: to confirm or support a decision, especially a legal one
vocabulary.excerpt: Supreme Court Upholds Bans on Transgender Athletes.
vocabulary.example_cn: 最高法院维持对跨性别运动员的禁令。

vocabulary.en: denoting or relating to a person whose gender identity does not correspond with the sex registered at birth
vocabulary.excerpt: Supreme Court Upholds Bans on Transgender Athletes.
vocabulary.example_cn: 最高法院维持对跨性别运动员的禁令。

vocabulary.en: a large, destructive fire that spreads quickly over woodland or brush
vocabulary.excerpt: Western wildfires explode, scorching thousands of acres.
vocabulary.example_cn: 西部野火爆发，烧毁数千英亩。

vocabulary.en: to burn the surface of something with intense heat
vocabulary.excerpt: Western wildfires explode, scorching thousands of acres.
vocabulary.example_cn: 西部野火爆发，烧毁数千英亩。

vocabulary.cn: 自由球员（合同到期可自由签约的运动员）
vocabulary.en: a professional athlete who is not under contract and is free to sign with any team
vocabulary.excerpt: LeBron James, a free agent. And almost every NBA team is sure to want him.
vocabulary.example_cn: 勒布朗·詹姆斯成为自由球员。几乎每支NBA球队都肯定想要他。

vocabulary.en: a failure or disappointment
vocabulary.excerpt: In Lakers' standards, it was -- it was a bust. It was a waste of money.
vocabulary.example_cn: 按照湖人的标准，那是一次失败。那是浪费钱。

vocabulary.en: a small group of people having control of a country or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: They said the establishment and the oligarchy is just too big and too powerful to overcome.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们说建制派和寡头集团太大、太强大，无法战胜。

vocabulary.cn: 有组织的人民 vs. 有组织的金钱（政治口号）
vocabulary.en: a phrase contrasting grassroots mobilization with financial power
vocabulary.excerpt: You are the proof that the power of organized people beats the power of organized money.
vocabulary.example_cn: 你们就是证明：有组织的人民的力量战胜有组织的金钱的力量。

vocabulary.en: to succeed in overcoming an obstacle or limit
vocabulary.excerpt: We broke through a lot of negative ads. We broke through a lot of noise.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们突破了许多负面广告。我们突破了许多噪音。

vocabulary.en: conflict within a group or organization
vocabulary.excerpt: back a few months ago when there was infighting in the DNC itself
vocabulary.example_cn: 几个月前，当民主党全国委员会内部出现内斗时

vocabulary.cn: 民主党全国委员会 (Democratic National Committee)
vocabulary.en: the governing body of the United States Democratic Party
vocabulary.excerpt: back a few months ago when there was infighting in the DNC itself
vocabulary.example_cn: 几个月前，当民主党全国委员会内部出现内斗时

vocabulary.en: to evoke or suggest images, memories, or emotions
vocabulary.excerpt: I do think the themes in these races speak to -- they resonate with something broader in the Democratic base across the country.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我确实认为这些竞选的主题与全国民主党基础选民中更广泛的东西产生了共鸣。

vocabulary.cn: 代际竞选（年轻挑战者与年长在位者之间的竞选）
vocabulary.en: an election contest between candidates of different generations
vocabulary.excerpt: You just highlighted somebody who won a generational race, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: 你刚刚强调了一位赢得代际竞选的候选人，对吧？

vocabulary.en: the deliberate killing of a large number of people from a particular nation or ethnic group
vocabulary.excerpt: We will not wait to put an end to the politics of the past; to get big money out of our politics; and to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC. And no, we will not wait to end the genocide in Palestine.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们不会等待结束过去的政治；将大笔资金赶出我们的政治；拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。不，我们不会等待结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝。

vocabulary.cn: 美国以色列公共事务委员会 (American Israel Public Affairs Committee)
vocabulary.en: a pro-Israel lobbying group in the United States
vocabulary.excerpt: to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC.
vocabulary.example_cn: 拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。

sentences.en: And what we're seeing with this series of wins, the ones you just outlined, but also were coming a week after Mamdani's sweep in New York.
sentences.cn: 我们看到的这一系列胜利，不仅是你刚才提到的那些，而且是在曼达尼在纽约大获全胜一周后出现的。
sentences.structure: 主句为 what we're seeing...，后接同位语 the ones you just outlined，but also 引导并列结构，were coming 为谓语，a week after... 为时间状语。
sentences.analysis: what 引导主语从句；同位语 the ones 补充说明 wins；but also 连接并列谓语；sweep 为新闻常用词，指压倒性胜利。

sentences.en: I do think the themes in these races speak to -- they resonate with something broader in the Democratic base across the country.
sentences.cn: 我确实认为这些竞选的主题反映了——它们与全国民主党基础中更广泛的东西产生共鸣。
sentences.structure: 主句 I do think，后接宾语从句 the themes...speak to，破折号后为补充说明的同位语从句 they resonate with...。
sentences.analysis: do 强调谓语；speak to 意为“反映，表明”；resonate with 意为“与……共鸣”；broader 为比较级，强调范围更广。

sentences.en: In the last presidential campaign, I would often hear from Democratic voters they didn't feel people represented them.
sentences.cn: 在上次总统竞选中，我经常听到民主党选民说，他们觉得没有人代表他们。
sentences.structure: 时间状语 In the last presidential campaign 前置，主句 I would often hear from Democratic voters，后接省略 that 的宾语从句 they didn't feel people represented them。
sentences.analysis: would 表示过去习惯性动作；hear from 意为“收到……的来信/消息”；feel 后接宾语从句，省略 that；represented 为过去式。

sentences.en: She was born a year after the incumbent won her first race.
sentences.cn: 她是在现任议员赢得第一次竞选一年后出生的。
sentences.structure: 主句 She was born，后接时间状语从句 a year after the incumbent won her first race。
sentences.analysis: after 引导时间状语从句；incumbent 指现任议员；race 在此指竞选。

sentences.en: And the issue of whether the Democratic Party will support U.S. support for Israel, that has resonated both in New York and these races.
sentences.cn: 而民主党是否会支持美国对以色列的支持这一问题，在纽约和这些竞选中都引起了共鸣。
sentences.structure: 主语 the issue of whether...，后接同位语从句 whether the Democratic Party will support...，主句谓语 has resonated，宾语 both in New York and these races。
sentences.analysis: whether 引导同位语从句；support 出现两次，注意区分：第一个 support 为动词，第二个为名词；resonate 意为“引起共鸣”。

sentences.en: I think what happened with Kamala Harris and Biden is they truly struggled to talk about Israel and Gaza when that fight was at its height.
sentences.cn: 我认为卡玛拉·哈里斯和拜登的情况是，当那场战斗最激烈时，他们确实很难谈论以色列和加沙。
sentences.structure: 主句 I think，后接宾语从句 what happened...is they truly struggled...，其中 what 引导主语从句，is 后为表语从句（省略 that），when 引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: what 引导主语从句；struggled to do 意为“努力做某事，艰难地做”；at its height 意为“在最激烈/最高峰时”。

sentences.en: When I wrote a letter defending students' rights to protest the genocide in Gaza, my law firm told me, Take it down or you're fired.
sentences.cn: 当我写一封捍卫学生抗议加沙种族灭绝权利的信时，我的律师事务所告诉我，要么撤下它，要么你被解雇。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句 When I wrote...，主句 my law firm told me，后接直接引语 Take it down or you're fired。
sentences.analysis: defending 为现在分词作定语修饰 letter；genocide 意为“种族灭绝”；Take it down or you're fired 为祈使句+并列句，表示威胁。

sentences.en: We will not wait to put an end to the politics of the past; to get big money out of our politics; and to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC.
sentences.cn: 我们不会等待结束过去的政治；将大笔资金从我们的政治中剔除；并拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。
sentences.structure: 主句 We will not wait，后接三个并列的不定式短语 to put an end to...; to get...; and to reject...。
sentences.analysis: wait to do 意为“等待做某事”；put an end to 意为“结束”；get...out of 意为“把……从……中剔除”；corporate PACs 指企业政治行动委员会；AIPAC 为美国以色列公共事务委员会。

sentences.en: And no, we will not wait to end the genocide in Palestine.
sentences.cn: 不，我们不会等待结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝。
sentences.structure: 插入语 And no 后接主句 we will not wait to end...。
sentences.analysis: no 为独立成分，表示强调否定；wait to end 意为“等待结束”；genocide 再次出现，强调立场。

sentences.en: I think the common denominator is that people are frustrated.
sentences.cn: 我认为共同点是人们感到沮丧。
sentences.structure: 主句 I think，后接宾语从句 the common denominator is that...，其中 that 引导表语从句。
sentences.analysis: common denominator 原为数学术语“公分母”，此处比喻“共同点”；frustrated 意为“沮丧的”。

sentences.en: You can talk boldly and loudly about all of these policies, but if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate.
sentences.cn: 你可以大胆而响亮地谈论所有这些政策，但如果你不能执政，如果你不赢，而失败者不能立法。
sentences.structure: 并列句：前半句 You can talk...，but 后为条件状语从句 if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate。
sentences.analysis: boldly and loudly 为并列副词修饰 talk；govern 意为“执政”；legislate 意为“立法”；注意最后一句为独立分句，与前面 if 从句并列。

sentences.en: And when you talk about the way that some of our more progressive candidates who are winning in deep-blue districts, the issues they are talking about are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe if they want to respond to what they campaign.
sentences.cn: 当你谈到我们一些更进步的候选人在深蓝选区获胜的方式时，他们谈论的问题将要求他们必须在自己信仰的事情上妥协，如果他们想回应他们竞选时所承诺的。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句 when you talk about the way...，其中 way 后接 that 引导的定语从句，定语从句中嵌套 who 引导的定语从句修饰 candidates；主句 the issues...are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe，后接 if 条件状语从句，其中 what they campaign 为宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: deep-blue districts 指民主党占绝对优势的选区；require sb. to do 意为“要求某人做某事”；compromise on 意为“在……上妥协”；respond to 意为“回应”；campaign 在此为动词，意为“竞选，宣传”。

sentences.en: I think we've put these labels on folks, and I think that has been part of the somewhat division you see within the primaries.
sentences.cn: 我认为我们给人们贴上了这些标签，而且我认为这已经成为你在初选中看到的那种分裂的一部分。
sentences.structure: 并列句：I think we've put...，and I think that has been...，其中 that 指代前句内容，you see within the primaries 为省略 that 的定语从句修饰 division。
sentences.analysis: put labels on 意为“给……贴标签”；somewhat 为副词，修饰 division，意为“有点，某种程度上的”；primaries 指初选。

sentences.en: At the end of the day, when you go into the United States Congress, you're labeled as a Democrat versus Republican.
sentences.cn: 归根结底，当你进入美国国会时，你被贴上民主党或共和党的标签。
sentences.structure: 时间状语 At the end of the day 和 when you go into...，主句 you're labeled as...。
sentences.analysis: At the end of the day 为固定短语，意为“最终，说到底”；labeled as 意为“被贴上……的标签”；versus 意为“对，与……相对”。

sentences.en: I think this is probably true in Republican primaries.
sentences.cn: 我认为这在共和党初选中可能也是如此。
sentences.structure: 主句 I think，后接宾语从句 this is probably true...。
sentences.analysis: probably 表示可能性；true 意为“真实的，正确的”；in Republican primaries 为地点状语。

sentences.en: It's easier to vote against something than it is to vote for something.
sentences.cn: 投票反对某件事比投票支持某件事更容易。
sentences.structure: 形式主语 It，真正主语 to vote against something，than 引导比较状语从句 it is to vote for something。
sentences.analysis: It's easier to do A than to do B 为比较结构；vote against 意为“投票反对”；vote for 意为“投票支持”。

sentences.en: And who they feel like a leader.
sentences.cn: 以及他们觉得谁是领导者。
sentences.structure: 省略主句的从句，完整应为 and (people are voting for) who they feel like a leader，其中 who 引导宾语从句。
sentences.analysis: feel like 在此意为“觉得是”；leader 意为“领导者”。

sentences.en: I'm looking at like, for instance, the "Post" response to the Supreme Court upholding bans on trans athletes.
sentences.cn: 比如，我正在看《华盛顿邮报》对最高法院维持跨性别运动员禁令的回应。
sentences.structure: 主句 I'm looking at，插入语 like, for instance，宾语 the "Post" response，后接介词短语 to the Supreme Court upholding bans on trans athletes。
sentences.analysis: look at 意为“看”；upholding 为动名词，作介词 to 的宾语，意为“维持，支持”；bans on 意为“对……的禁令”；trans 为 transgender 的缩写。

sentences.en: This kind of follows what he's saying.
sentences.cn: 这有点符合他所说的。
sentences.structure: 主句 This kind of follows，后接宾语从句 what he's saying。
sentences.analysis: kind of 为口语表达，意为“有点，某种程度上”；follow 意为“遵循，符合”。

sentences.en: It's easy to say no.
sentences.cn: 说“不”很容易。
sentences.structure: 形式主语 It，真正主语 to say no。
sentences.analysis: It's easy to do 结构；say no 意为“说不，拒绝”。

topics.title: 民主党初选中的进步派崛起
topics.content: 2026年科罗拉多州民主党初选中，进步派候选人梅拉特·基罗斯（Melat Kiros）击败连任近30年的温和派众议员戴安娜·德盖特（Diana DeGette），州总检察长菲尔·韦泽（Phil Weiser）击败参议员迈克尔·贝内特（Michael Bennet）。这反映了民主党内部代际和意识形态分歧加剧，进步派通过反对建制派、反对AIPAC和以色列政策、主张全民医保等议题动员年轻选民。Justice Democrats等组织在多地初选中获胜，挑战传统民主党领导层。
topics.keywords: Democratic primary · progressive · establishment · Justice Democrats · AIPAC

topics.title: 最高法院维持跨性别运动员禁令
topics.content: 美国最高法院裁定维持对跨性别运动员参加女子体育比赛的禁令。这一裁决被视为保守派法院的立场体现，支持者认为保护了女子体育的公平性，反对者则批评其歧视跨性别群体。该议题成为文化战争焦点，影响2026年中期选举。
topics.keywords: Supreme Court · transgender athletes · ban · uphold · conservative court

topics.title: 出生公民权争议与特朗普盟友的挑战
topics.content: 最高法院维持了出生公民权（birthright citizenship），即在美国出生即自动获得公民身份的权利。特朗普及其盟友曾试图通过行政命令废除该权利，但遭遇法律障碍。此次裁决后，特朗普盟友誓言继续通过其他方式限制移民，包括推动立法或修改宪法解释。
topics.keywords: birthright citizenship · Trump allies · Supreme Court · immigration · executive order

topics.title: 西部野火与极端天气
topics.content: 美国西部多州爆发大规模野火，烧毁数千英亩土地。气象部门警告，本周后期可能出现更危险的天气条件，如强风和干燥雷暴，加剧火势。野火季节提前且强度增加，与气候变化相关，给消防和应急管理带来巨大压力。
topics.keywords: wildfire · Western US · scorch · extreme weather · climate change

quiz.question: 在原文 'Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate.' 中，'moderate' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 在政治语境中，'moderate' 作为名词指政治立场温和的人，即温和派。原文中与 'Democratic socialist' 对比，指击败了一位温和派候选人。

quiz.question: 短语 'organized people beats the power of organized money' 中，'organized money' 指的是？
options: B. 大财团和利益集团的政治献金
quiz.explanation: 在政治语境中，'organized money' 指通过政治行动委员会（PACs）、企业捐赠等方式集中使用的资金，代表建制派和既得利益集团的力量。

quiz.question: 原文 'A rare rebuke from the conservative court.' 中，'rebuke' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'Rebuke' 意为严厉批评或谴责。这里指保守派占多数的最高法院罕见地批评了政府或某项政策，结合上下文是维持出生公民权，对特朗普政府构成打击。

quiz.question: 根据文章，梅拉特·基罗斯（Melat Kiros）在竞选中主要反对什么？
options: B. 以色列政策和AIPAC的影响
quiz.explanation: 基罗斯在胜选演讲中明确表示要拒绝企业PAC和AIPAC，并结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝，表明她反对以色列现行政策和AIPAC的游说影响力。

quiz.question: 分析人士认为进步派候选人在初选中获胜的主要原因是什么？
options: A. 他们获得了更多资金支持
B. 他们代表了选民对建制派的不满和代际更替
C. 他们得到了共和党的支持
D. 他们专注于地方议题
quiz.explanation: 文章指出，选民感到被建制派忽视，进步派候选人通过反对金钱政治、以色列政策等议题，以及年轻一代的活力，赢得了对现状不满的选民支持。

quiz.question: 关于最高法院对跨性别运动员禁令的裁决，文章暗示了什么？
options: A. 裁决得到了两党一致支持
B. 裁决反映了保守派法院的立场
C. 裁决将被立即推翻
D. 裁决与出生公民权案件无关
quiz.explanation: 文章称该裁决为 'rare rebuke from the conservative court'，暗示最高法院保守派多数做出了一个不寻常的批评性裁决，维持了禁令，体现了其保守倾向。

paragraphs.speaker: Brad Smith
paragraphs.text: That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, do Democrats see progressives as a problem? Another Democratic socialist knocks out a moderate. Is this something establishment Dems need to start worrying about? MELAT KIROS (D),
paragraphs.cn: 今天的群聊话题：民主党人是否将进步派视为问题？又一位民主社会主义者击败了一位温和派。这是否是建制派民主党人需要开始担忧的事情？梅拉特·基罗斯（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Congressional Candidate
paragraphs.text: From coast to coast, from every level of office, we are taking back our party and our country.
paragraphs.cn: 从东海岸到西海岸，从各级公职，我们正在夺回我们的政党和我们的国家。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A rare rebuke from the conservative court. Birthright citizenship upheld. How the president wants to get his way anyway. And Western wildfires explode, scorching thousands of acres. The short relief before even more dangerous weather later this week. And LeBron James, a free agent. And almost every NBA team is sure to want him. Some Lakers fans aren't so sad to see him go.
paragraphs.cn: 来自保守派法院的罕见斥责。出生公民权得到维持。总统无论如何都想达成目的。西部野火爆发，烧毁数千英亩土地。在本周晚些时候更危险的天气到来之前，短暂的缓解。勒布朗·詹姆斯成为自由球员。几乎每支NBA球队都肯定想要他。一些湖人球迷对他的离开并不那么难过。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: In Lakers' standards, it was -- it was a bust. It was a waste of money.
paragraphs.cn: 按照湖人的标准，这 --  -- 这是一个失败。这是浪费钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Kiros
paragraphs.text: They said the establishment and the oligarchy is just too big and too powerful to overcome. You are the proof that the power of organized people beats the power of organized money.
paragraphs.cn: 他们说建制派和寡头政治过于庞大和强大，无法战胜。你证明了有组织的人民力量胜过有组织的金钱力量。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A warning shot for establishment Dems. Two longtime prominent Democrats are now out. Good morning, everybody. And here's where we begin. Stunning losses in Colorado. State attorney general Phil Weiser upsets Senator Michael Bennet in the Democratic primary for governor. PHIL WEISER (D),
paragraphs.cn: 对建制派民主党人的警告。两位长期知名的民主党人现已出局。大家早上好。我们从这里开始。科罗拉多州令人震惊的失利。州总检察长菲尔·韦泽在民主党州长初选中击败了参议员迈克尔·贝内特。菲尔·韦泽（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: State Attorney General
paragraphs.text: People want to know that they're electing someone who's in this work to fight for them, who they know cares about them. And the goal of our campaign was to break through. We broke through a lot of negative ads. We broke through a lot of noise, because I believe in the power of people.
paragraphs.cn: 人们想知道他们选举的是为他们奋斗的人，是关心他们的人。我们竞选的目标是取得突破。我们突破了许多负面广告。我们突破了许多噪音，因为我相信人民的力量。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then you had Melat Kiros beat a nearly 30-year incumbent congresswoman, Diana DeGette, last night. Kiros, the latest Democratic socialist to win their primary.
paragraphs.cn: 然后昨晚梅拉特·基罗斯击败了任职近30年的现任女议员戴安娜·德盖特。基罗斯是最新一位赢得初选的民主社会主义者。

paragraphs.speaker: Kiros
paragraphs.text: We believe that fundamental change can and will happen if we fight for it; if we organize and show no fear in standing up for what's right. That is the message that Denver has sent to both parties, to Donald Trump, and to the entire country.
paragraphs.cn: 我们相信，如果我们为之奋斗，如果我们组织起来，毫无畏惧地捍卫正义，根本性的改变就能够而且将会发生。这是丹佛向两党、向唐纳德·特朗普以及向整个国家传达的信息。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, today in the group chat, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, CNN political analyst and White House correspondent with "The New York Times"; Terry Schilling, president of the American Principles Project; and Antjuan Seawright, Democratic strategist. Zolan, I want to start with you, because back a few months ago when there was infighting in the DNC itself, there were all these people that were like, I'm taking my toys and going home. Right? Like David Hogg, I'm going to go spend my own money, just as Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，今天的群聊中，有CNN政治分析师、《纽约时报》白宫记者佐兰·坎诺-扬斯；美国原则项目主席特里·席林；以及民主党策略师安特万·西赖特。佐兰，我想先请你谈谈，因为几个月前，当民主党全国委员会内部出现内讧时，有很多人就像在说，我要拿上我的玩具回家了。对吧？比如大卫·霍格，我要花自己的钱，就像民主党人一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We're going to recruit our own people. There was this real sense that there was going to be money spent elsewhere by different recruiters for different reasons. And I want to show the viewers right now how well justice Democrats are doing, as it turns out, primary winners. I think I'm looking at a count of up to eight faces here. What are they doing differently than the DNC?
paragraphs.cn: 我们要招募我们自己的人。当时确实有一种感觉，不同的招募者会出于不同的原因把钱花到别处。我想向观众展示一下正义民主党人现在做得有多好，事实证明，他们在初选中获胜了。我想我数了数，这里有多达八张面孔。他们和民主党全国委员会的做法有什么不同？

paragraphs.speaker: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: Yes. It's interesting, because that fight over spending, I do think, represented a broader fight over the direction of the Democratic Party --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这很有趣，因为我认为，关于支出的这场斗争代表了关于民主党方向的一场更广泛的斗争 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: -- and who should lead them. And what we're seeing with this series of wins, the ones you just outlined, but also were coming a week after Mamdani's --
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 以及谁应该领导他们。我们看到的这一系列胜利，就是你刚才列举的那些，而且是在马姆达尼 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And those are in --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。那些是在 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: -- sweep in New York.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 在纽约大获全胜之后一周发生的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- Colorado, New Jersey, California.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 科罗拉多州、新泽西州、加利福尼亚州。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: Right, right. Across the country. I do think the themes in these races speak to -- they resonate with something broader in the Democratic base across the country. Right? In the last presidential campaign, I would often hear from Democratic voters. They didn't feel people represented them. You just highlighted somebody who won a generational race, right?
paragraphs.cn: 对，对。遍布全国。我确实认为这些竞选中的主题与全国民主党基础选民中更广泛的东西产生了共鸣。对吧？在上一次总统竞选中，我经常听到民主党选民说，他们觉得没有人代表他们。你刚才提到有人赢得了一场代际竞选，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: She was born a year after the incumbent won her first race.
paragraphs.cn: 她是在现任议员赢得首次竞选一年后出生的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But with the views that come with that, in some cases.
paragraphs.cn: 但在某些情况下，也伴随着随之而来的观点。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: And that brings -- brings us to Israel, as well.
paragraphs.cn: 这也把我们带到了以色列。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: We have seen Israel. And the issue of whether the Democratic Party will support U.S. support for Israel, that has resonated both in New York and these races.
paragraphs.cn: 我们看到了以色列的情况。民主党是否会支持美国对以色列的支持，这个问题在纽约和这些竞选中引起了共鸣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. I think I'm also fascinated by that, because what happened with Kamala Harris and Biden is they truly struggled to talk about Israel and Gaza when that fight was at its height. Then you have someone like Melat Kiros. I want to play one more thing from her, this Colorado congressional candidate who had a win, because she uses an argument that you wouldn't have heard from the Biden administration last fall.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我想我也对此很感兴趣，因为卡玛拉·哈里斯和拜登在冲突最激烈时确实难以谈论以色列和加沙。然后像梅拉特·基罗斯这样的人出现了。我想再播放一段她的内容，这位科罗拉多州的国会候选人取得了胜利，因为她使用的论点是去年秋天拜登政府不会说的。

paragraphs.speaker: Kiros
paragraphs.text: When I wrote a letter defending students' rights to protest the genocide in Gaza, my law firm -- my law firm told me, Take it down or you're fired. I didn't flinch. We will not wait to put an end to the politics of the past; to get big money out of our politics; and to reject corporate PACs and AIPAC. And no, we will not wait to end the genocide in Palestine.
paragraphs.cn: 当我写一封信捍卫学生抗议加沙种族灭绝的权利时，我的律师事务所告诉我，要么撤下，要么被解雇。我没有退缩。我们不会等待结束过去的政治；不会等待把大笔资金从政治中剔除；不会等待拒绝企业政治行动委员会和AIPAC。不，我们不会等待结束巴勒斯坦的种族灭绝。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, every key word that's a problem for Democrats and a problem, especially, for people who are concerned about the rhetoric around Israel, punctuated by an actual air horn. I want to play one more thing. A voter, and then, Antjuan, I want you to talk out of this.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，每一个对民主党来说是个问题，特别是对那些关心围绕以色列言论的人来说是个问题的关键词，都被一个真正的汽笛声打断。我想再播放一段。一位选民，然后，安特万，我希望你对此发表看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: I like to see that she has more socialist ideals.
paragraphs.cn: 我喜欢看到她有更多的社会主义理想。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I think people are tired of our tax dollars being spent overseas and on bombs to kill children.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为人们厌倦了我们的税款被花在海外和用来制造炸弹杀害儿童。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I'm anti-AIPAC, and so I just wanted to vote for someone that was standing up against Israel. And I knew that the incumbent was for Israel. So voted against her.
paragraphs.cn: 我反对AIPAC，所以我只想投票给一个反对以色列的人。我知道现任议员支持以色列，所以投票反对她。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: People like Kiros and the DSA in general are presenting more, like, compelling arguments about how to make this country better for everyone.
paragraphs.cn: 像基罗斯这样的人和DSA总体上提出了更有说服力的论点，关于如何让这个国家变得对每个人都更好。

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.text: A couple of things. We're having a generational conversation and an ideological conversation in these primaries. I think the common denominator is that people are frustrated. People are pissed off. And we collective Democrats are against Donald Trump and the Republican majority. How we talk about these issues has defined these primaries. And -- But I will make the continued argument that the most important thing for Democrats to remember is 218 is the name of the game.
paragraphs.cn: 有几点。在这些初选中，我们正在进行一场代际对话和意识形态对话。我认为共同点是人们感到沮丧。人们很生气。我们民主党人共同反对唐纳德·特朗普和共和党多数派。我们如何谈论这些问题定义了这些初选。但我要继续论证，民主党人需要记住的最重要的事情是218是游戏的名字。

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.text: You can talk boldly and loudly about all of these policies, but if you cannot govern, if you do not win, and losers do not legislate. And when you talk about the way that some of our more progressive candidates who are winning in deep-blue districts, the issues they are talking about are going to require them to have to compromise on what they believe if they want to respond to what they campaign.
paragraphs.cn: 你可以大胆而响亮地谈论所有这些政策，但如果你不能治理，如果你不能获胜，失败者是无法立法的。当你谈到我们一些在深蓝选区获胜的更进步的候选人时，他们谈论的问题将要求他们在想要回应竞选承诺时，必须在自己信仰上做出妥协。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Require them or require you and the rest of the establishment, because that's where the money is going to have to come from, probably?
paragraphs.cn: 要求他们还是要求你和建制派其他人，因为资金可能必须从那里来？

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Well, I don't consider myself establishment. I consider myself just a Democrat. I think we've put these labels on folks, and I think that has been part of the somewhat division you see within the primaries. At the end of the day, when you go into the United States Congress, you're labeled as a Democrat versus Republican. We should anchor that there. But the second point is what we're seeing across the board. I think this is probably true in Republican primaries.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我不认为自己是建制派。我认为自己只是一个民主党人。我觉得我们给人们贴上了这些标签，而这正是你在初选中看到某种分裂的部分原因。归根结底，当你进入美国国会时，你被贴上的是民主党人还是共和党人的标签。我们应该以此为锚点。但第二点是我们在各地看到的。我认为这在共和党初选中可能也是如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: It's easier to vote against something than it is to vote for something. So, people are voting for who are -- who has been in charge --
paragraphs.cn: 投票反对某件事比投票支持某件事更容易。所以，人们投票给那些 --  -- 谁一直在掌权 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: And who they feel like a leader.
paragraphs.cn: 以及他们觉得谁是领导者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Terry, can I have the answer to that, especially to this point? I'm looking at like, for instance, the "Post" response to the Supreme Court upholding bans on trans athletes. This kind of follows what he's saying. It's easy to say no. "No" is easy to put in a headline. It's easy to put it in ad.
paragraphs.cn: 特里，我能回答这个问题吗，尤其是针对这一点？我想到的是，比如《华盛顿邮报》对最高法院维持跨性别运动员禁令的回应。这和他说的类似。说"不"很容易。"不"很容易上头条，也很容易放进广告里。

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.text: No, that's exactly right, Audie. And I think that candidates like this, that are more radical, where the mask has come off, they're winning in deep blue districts, right? Zohran Mamdani is not running in Virginia. He's running in New York City.
paragraphs.cn: 不，完全正确，奥迪。我认为像这样更激进的候选人，当面具被摘下时，他们在深蓝选区获胜，对吧？佐兰·马姆达尼不是在弗吉尼亚州竞选。他在纽约市竞选。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: And the same thing here. It's going to be interesting to see how these new, more radically left-wing candidates fare in terms of the -- the upcoming midterms. But it's also going to be interesting to see how they change the coalition for the Democratic Party. When President Trump came on the scene back, you know, ten years ago, he changed the makeup of the Republican Party.
paragraphs.cn: 同样的情况。看看这些新的、更激进的左翼候选人在即将到来的中期选举中表现如何，将会很有趣。但同样有趣的是，他们将如何改变民主党的联盟。当特朗普总统大约十年前登场时，他改变了共和党的构成。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: And I could see a lot of these higher propensity voters --
paragraphs.cn: 我可以看到很多这些高倾向选民 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I play an example?
paragraphs.cn: 我能举个例子吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Here's an example of someone who thrives in the Trump era. The Colorado candidate for governor on the Republican side. These are the kinds of questions and back and forth he's dealing. I just want to play this for the audience before we go.
paragraphs.cn: 这里有一个在特朗普时代如鱼得水的例子。科罗拉多州的共和党州长候选人。他正在应对的就是这类问题和交锋。在节目结束前，我想让观众听听这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Your campaign website falsely claimed that you had rescued more than 45,000 women and children. You claim that you once called in a U.S. military airstrike that killed 70 ISIS fighters. You perform exorcisms, commanding demons to come out of people. Your claim that your abusive stepfather forced you to kill a man when you were 7 years old, is that the only person you've ever killed? VICTOR MARX (R),

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.text: Well, I would say as a -- as a child, yes.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我想说，作为一个孩子，是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It feels like the takeaway for Republicans has not been radicals, don't need them.
paragraphs.cn: 感觉共和党人得到的教训并不是激进分子，不需要他们。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It seems like it's, like, certain people are more than encouraged by the current environment.
paragraphs.cn: 看起来，某些人反而受到当前环境的鼓励。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: I would -- I would -- I would ignore people like this.
paragraphs.cn: 我会 --  -- 我会 --  -- 我会无视这种人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Why? He's running for governor.
paragraphs.cn: 为什么？他正在竞选州长。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: No, I know, but I'm from Illinois. Right? And so -- so what happens in these deep blue states where, like, Republicans basically give up on is it's not --
paragraphs.cn: 不，我知道，但我来自伊利诺伊州。对吧？所以，在这些深蓝州，共和党人基本上放弃的地方，发生的事情并不是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Which is not Colorado, by the way. It is not deep blue, but go on.
paragraphs.cn: 顺便说一句，科罗拉多州不是深蓝州。但请继续。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Colorado is pretty blue, Audie. But I look, I would -- I would look more to the states like Virginia and New Jersey.
paragraphs.cn: 科罗拉多州相当蓝，奥黛。但我会更关注像弗吉尼亚州和新泽西州这样的州。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But then why run a guy like that?
paragraphs.cn: 但既然如此，为什么还要推这样的人参选？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: We -- well, look, because a lot of the serious people are not running in Colorado. If I was a serious candidate for GOP governor --
paragraphs.cn: 我们 --  -- 嗯，你看，因为很多严肃的人并没有在科罗拉多州参选。如果我是共和党州长的严肃候选人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: Not just run a guy, but he's leading in donations in that race, as well.
paragraphs.cn: 不只是推人参选，而且他在那场竞选中收到的捐款也领先。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: there's money donors, probably not from Colorado, are giving him money.
paragraphs.cn: 有捐款人，可能不是来自科罗拉多州，在给他钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: And what -- and what I will say is that the -- the -- the people, the actual voters, they feel like both establishments are screwing them over. They feel like they're getting behind further and further. And that's the Democrats and the Republicans. And the first candidate that actually addresses these concerns in a real way is going to be the winner.
paragraphs.cn: 我要说的是 --  -- 选民们，真正的选民，他们觉得两党都在欺骗他们。他们觉得自己越来越落后。无论是民主党还是共和党。第一个真正解决这些问题的候选人将会获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. Well, sorry, this one is really -- I'm befuddled. So, I want to know more, especially -- well, because where the money goes is where they think there is money well spent. I want to come back to the Supreme Court, though. They have rejected the president's efforts to end birthright citizenship. But the White House isn't giving up. They're going to try to come up with some other way to bring this back.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。抱歉，这个真的让我很困惑。所以我想了解更多，尤其是 --  -- 因为钱流向哪里，就说明他们认为钱花得值。不过，我想回到最高法院的话题。他们驳回了总统终止出生公民权的努力。但白宫没有放弃。他们打算想出其他办法来重新提出这个问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then we want to talk about the children in Venezuela who have been pulled alive from the rubble after that earthquake. One other thing. The queen is back. Serena Williams returns to Wimbledon, her first singles match in four years. She did lose to Australian Maya Joint, who spoke after the match.
paragraphs.cn: 然后我们想谈谈委内瑞拉地震后从废墟中救出的孩子们。还有一件事。女王回来了。塞雷娜·威廉姆斯重返温布尔登，这是她四年来首场单打比赛。她输给了澳大利亚选手玛雅·乔因特，后者在赛后发表了讲话。

paragraphs.speaker: Maya Joint
paragraphs.text: She has such an aura. She's such a legend, and this court has so many huge names that have played on it. I just -- yes, I was -- I've been dreaming about this moment since I was a little kid.
paragraphs.cn: 她气场太强大了。她是个传奇，这片球场上有过太多大牌球员。我只是 --  -- 是的，我 --  -- 我从小就一直梦想着这一刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the Supreme Court now on summer vacation, wrapping up their latest term Tuesday after handing down 58 opinions, including a dozen major rulings. Among those final rulings, a 6-to-3 decision which struck down President Trump's effort to end birthright citizenship via an executive order. The president's allies are already vowing to reverse the decision. REP. MIKE JOHNSON (R-LA): It certainly is time for us to do everything that's possible. I don't know what that is.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，最高法院现在放暑假了，周二结束了最新一个任期，共发布了58份意见书，其中包括十几项重大裁决。在最后一批裁决中，有一项6比3的裁决，推翻了特朗普总统通过行政命令终止出生公民权的努力。总统的盟友已经誓言要推翻这一裁决。共和党众议员迈克·约翰逊：当然是时候尽一切可能了。我不知道那是什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I will tell you that it's a big concern for the American people, because it's been abused.
paragraphs.cn: 我要告诉你，这是美国人民非常关心的问题，因为它被滥用了。

paragraphs.speaker: J.D. Vance
paragraphs.text: We have to keep fighting, Laura, because we actually have an opportunity to reverse this decision, just as we've reversed so many bad decisions throughout the generations.
paragraphs.cn: 我们必须继续战斗，劳拉，因为我们实际上有机会推翻这一决定，就像我们在几代人以来推翻了许多糟糕的决定一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Joining the group chat, Steve Vladeck, CNN Supreme Court analyst and professor at the Georgetown University Law Center. I want to talk about these two hot-button ones. First, birthright citizenship and also upholding the ban on transgender youth in sports. What is it exactly that the court was arguing about in the end on this birthright citizenship, because it was actually closer than some legal watchers thought it would be?
paragraphs.cn: 好的。加入群聊的是史蒂夫·弗拉德克，CNN最高法院分析师和乔治城大学法律中心教授。我想谈谈这两个热点问题。首先是出生公民权，以及维持对跨性别青少年参与体育运动的禁令。最高法院在出生公民权问题上最终争论的到底是什么？因为实际上它比一些法律观察人士预期的要接近。

paragraphs.speaker: Steve Vladeck
paragraphs.text: It was, Audie. I mean, so there are two different things going on in the birthright citizenship case. It's a six-justice majority that holds that the president's executive order itself is unlawful. And so, it's Chief Justice Roberts. It's Justice Amy Coney Barrett, Justice Brett Kavanaugh, and the three Democratic appointees. But Kavanaugh actually broke from the majority on the constitutional question: whether Congress might actually be able to change the rules for birthright citizenship.
paragraphs.cn: 确实如此，奥迪。我的意思是，出生公民权案件中有两件不同的事情。六位大法官的多数意见认为总统的行政命令本身是非法的。所以，首席大法官罗伯茨、大法官艾米·科尼·巴雷特、布雷特·卡瓦诺以及三位民主党任命的大法官。但卡瓦诺实际上在宪法问题上与多数意见分道扬镳：国会是否真的能够改变出生公民权的规则。

paragraphs.speaker: Steve Vladeck
paragraphs.text: On that question, an interpretation of the 14th Amendment, it was only 5 to 4, which I think was a lot closer than folks expected, not just before this case really got to the Supreme Court, but even after the oral argument back in April. Now, 5-4 is still not a majority. And so that doesn't mean --
paragraphs.cn: 在那个问题上，对第十四修正案的解释，只有5比4，我认为这比人们预期的要接近得多，不仅是在这个案件真正到达最高法院之前，甚至在四月份的口头辩论之后也是如此。现在，5比4仍然不是多数。所以这并不意味着 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But it's opening a door. If you're a lawmaker or somebody who's really into this, you're like, well, what's that? You know, is that language I can use? I just want to show where the polling is on this. "New York Times" reporting 55 percent of Americans support birthright citizenship. And then they mentioned this, that underneath the surface, there's a division between MAGA-identified Republicans and -- am I allowed to say establishment Republicans? Maybe? Antjuan?
paragraphs.cn: 但这打开了一扇门。如果你是一名立法者或对此非常感兴趣的人，你会想，嗯，那是什么？你知道，那是我可以使用的语言吗？我只想展示一下民意调查的结果。《纽约时报》报道55%的美国人支持出生公民权。然后他们提到，在表面之下，MAGA认同的共和党人和 --  -- 我可以说建制派共和党人吗？也许？安特万？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. And -- and wanting to push this forward. And I'm kind of curious about that. Is this something you campaign on or is this --
paragraphs.cn: 好的。并且 --  -- 想要推动这一点。我对此有点好奇。这是你竞选时主张的问题，还是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Well, look, I think there's an existential crisis with the Republican Party and with America right now in terms of immigration. We should be able to determine who can become American citizens. And I think that, ultimately, at the Supreme Court we've been dealing with is the highest levels of lawfare. The Supreme Court will hold up our laws and our chain (ph). Trump -- Trump has struggled to be able to fire the people in his own cabinet, in his own administration, who don't want to do the job he's doing.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，听着，我认为共和党和美国目前在移民问题上存在生存危机。我们应该能够决定谁可以成为美国公民。而且我认为，最终，我们在最高法院处理的是最高级别的法律战。最高法院将维护我们的法律和我们的链条。特朗普 --  -- 特朗普一直难以解雇他自己内阁和政府中那些不想做他工作的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I come to this? The Overton Window has moved -- a Washington phrase I despise. But it is true. We went from being like, yes, you're born here, you're a citizen, to now what was once a fringe legal thing --
paragraphs.cn: 我能插一句吗？奥弗顿窗口已经移动了 --  -- 一个我讨厌的华盛顿短语。但这是真的。我们从"是的，你出生在这里，你就是公民"变成了现在曾经是边缘法律问题的事情 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: Is one vote away from being constitutional law.
paragraphs.cn: 离成为宪法法律只差一票。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: I will say, though, the administration, when it comes to preparation for this issue, has mainly put the focus on messaging on it. I asked Pam Bondi at a press conference after an earlier Supreme Court hearing on this issue. President Trump and her appeared at the White House press briefing, and I asked them, what -- how will this actually work? Who will be verifying citizenship?
paragraphs.cn: 不过我要说，政府在准备这个问题时，主要把重点放在了信息传递上。在早些时候最高法院就这个问题举行听证会后的一次新闻发布会上，我向帕姆·邦迪提问。特朗普总统和她出现在白宫新闻发布会上，我问他们，这实际上将如何运作？谁来核实公民身份？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And we should be clear: the executive order would have said at least one parent has to be a citizen --
paragraphs.cn: 我们应该明确：行政令会规定，至少父母一方必须是公民 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- in order for a child to be legally considered.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 孩子才能被合法考虑。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: Or at least someone with -- with some kind of permanent immigration status.
paragraphs.cn: 或者至少是拥有某种永久移民身份的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: Right? And I think what --
paragraphs.cn: 对吧？我认为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right. Well, hold on one second. I want to stop with that one. Not because it's not interesting, but because we have two others that are far more controversial. And one of them is striking down the ban on transgender youth in sports. This, I want to play for you a piece of tape about how the lawyers for this are thinking about the road ahead. Specifically, these are the athletes who applaud the ban and the lawyer who argued for them.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，稍等一下。我想在这里停一下。不是因为这不有趣，而是因为我们还有两个更具争议的话题。其中之一是推翻禁止跨性别青少年参加体育运动的禁令。我想给你们播放一段录音，听听律师们如何看待未来的道路。具体来说，这些是支持该禁令的运动员以及为他们辩护的律师。

paragraphs.speaker: Madison Kenyon
paragraphs.text: My heart is full of gratitude for the attorney generals and for ADF and all their work on this, and to know that no female in the 27 states that have the protection will have to compete against a male like I did.
paragraphs.cn: 我满怀感激，感谢各州检察长、ADF以及他们为此所做的一切工作。知道在27个有保护措施的州，没有女性会像我一样不得不与男性竞争，这让我感到欣慰。

paragraphs.speaker: Kristen Waggoner
paragraphs.text: I do expect this decision eventually to impact the 23 states, because the court recognized that there are physical advantages that can exist, and we're already litigating in those states.
paragraphs.cn: 我确实预计这一裁决最终会影响其他23个州，因为法院承认存在生理优势，而我们已经在这些州提起诉讼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Donald Trump on social media calling this a big win. Can you briefly touch on what Kristen Waggoner, the attorney there -- she's from the Alliance Defending Freedom -- is saying? Is she saying the 23 states right now who allow transgender youth in sports better watch out, because there is legal language in here that can be used against them?
paragraphs.cn: 唐纳德·特朗普在社交媒体上称这是一场重大胜利。你能简要谈谈那里的律师克里斯汀·瓦格纳 --  -- 她来自捍卫自由联盟 --  -- 在说什么吗？她是否在说，目前允许跨性别青少年参加体育运动的23个州最好小心点，因为这里的法律语言可以用来对付他们？

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: So, I mean, I think she's saying that. I'm not sure she's right, which is to say, you know, the Supreme Court yesterday said it's not unconstitutional for states like West Virginia and Idaho to have a ban. But there's really nothing in there that says it would be unconstitutional to not.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为她是在说这个意思。但我不确定她是否正确，也就是说，你知道，最高法院昨天裁定，像西弗吉尼亚州和爱达荷州这样的州实施禁令并不违宪。但其中并没有任何内容表明不实施禁令就是违宪的。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: And so, I think what we're going to see for now, Audie, is that this issue really is something that's going to vary state by state until and unless you see, again, some kind of national legislation from Congress, which, you know, I don't think is something we'll hear any time soon.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，我认为我们目前将看到的是，奥黛，这个问题真的会因州而异，直到并且除非你再次看到国会出台某种全国性立法，而我认为这不会很快发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: If the bans are upheld, do you still make ads about it? Or is this a win, and you're done?
paragraphs.cn: 如果禁令被维持，你们还会就此做广告吗？还是说这算一场胜利，你们就此收手？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Yes. No, you have to make ads about it, because the goal of the Democratic Party is still to put gender identity into national and federal civil rights law. And if they keep pushing forward, that's what's going to happen all across the country. Those 27 states that worked very hard to protect girls' sports, those laws are all going to be wiped out. And boys will be able to compete in girls' sports wherever they want to.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。不，你必须就此做广告，因为民主党的目标仍然是将性别认同纳入国家和联邦民权法。如果他们继续推进，这将在全国范围内发生。那27个努力保护女子体育的州，那些法律都将被废除。男孩将能够随时随地参加女子体育比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: I'm not sure how the laws are going to be wiped out when the Supreme Court just said yesterday it doesn't violate federal anti- discrimination law.
paragraphs.cn: 我不确定这些法律将如何被废除，因为最高法院昨天刚刚裁定它不违反联邦反歧视法。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Well, because federal civil rights law trumps state -- any state law.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，因为联邦民权法高于州法 --  -- 任何州法。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: Right. And one of those laws was at issue in yesterday's case, and the Supreme Court specifically said -- in fact, on this point, the court was actually unanimous that Title IX, the federal anti-discrimination law from 1972, is not inconsistent with these bans. So, you know, I mean, I understand as a matter of politics, this is going to be something that gets everyone's dander up. I think yesterday's decision really does, perhaps even more so than Dobbs in the abortion context, return this question to the states.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。而昨天案件涉及的正是其中一项法律，最高法院特别指出 --  -- 事实上，在这一点上，法院实际上是一致的，即1972年的联邦反歧视法《教育法修正案第九条》与这些禁令并不矛盾。所以，你知道，我的意思是，从政治角度来看，这将成为激怒所有人的事情。我认为昨天的裁决确实，甚至可能比堕胎问题上的多布斯案更甚，将这个问题交还给了各州。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: The states are going to have different views on what the answer is.
paragraphs.cn: 各州对答案会有不同的看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: I think, broadly speaking, all of these court decisions are going to become political issues both in the fall and elections to come. When you talk about this fundamental truth, when it comes to a person being an American, being born in this country, being an American citizen, I think the Republicans are going to use that as an opportunity to make immigration a red-meat issue in the next couple of months, and a presidential election year. The same thing with the transgender or the girls and boys in sports.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，总的来说，所有这些法院裁决都将在秋季及未来的选举中成为政治议题。当你谈到这个基本事实 --  -- 一个人作为美国人、出生在这个国家、成为美国公民 --  -- 我认为共和党人将利用这个机会，在未来几个月和总统选举年将移民问题变成一个煽动性议题。跨性别或男女体育问题也是如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: And I think also, the campaign finance issue. I think all of these things are going to become campaign issues that both parties campaign on as a -- as a way to galvanize, energize their voters.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我认为，还有竞选资金问题。我认为所有这些都将成为竞选议题，两党都会以此作为动员和激励选民的方式。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I ask you that one last thing about that campaign finance bill? Because none of these issues matter if the money flowing in and out of politics can swing things one way or another without the voters' consent, effectively.
paragraphs.cn: 我能最后问你一个关于竞选资金法案的问题吗？因为如果流入和流出政治的资金可以在未经选民同意的情况下左右局势，那么所有这些议题都无关紧要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, what happened with this ruling?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，这项裁决的结果是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: I mean, this was the third ruling yesterday. It's gotten the least attention in some respects. It actually might be the most important in the short term. Because what the Supreme Court held is that the federal limits that go back to the 1970s on what are called coordinated expenditures, when a candidate coordinates their expenditures with the national party, those limits have been in place since 1976.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，这是昨天第三个裁决。在某些方面，它受到的关注最少。实际上，短期内它可能是最重要的。因为最高法院裁定，自20世纪70年代以来对所谓协调支出的联邦限制 --  -- 即候选人与全国政党协调支出时的限制 --  -- 这些限制自1976年以来一直存在。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: And the Supreme Court struck them down yesterday, even though, Audie, in 2001, the Supreme Court had specifically upheld them. What I think that really does is it opens the spigot in this cycle, especially for Republicans. But going forward, right, for individual candidates to basically draw from national pools of money without any constraint.
paragraphs.cn: 而最高法院昨天推翻了这些限制，尽管在2001年，最高法院曾明确支持这些限制。我认为这实际上打开了本轮选举的资金闸门，尤其是对共和党而言。但展望未来，个别候选人基本上可以不受约束地从全国资金池中提取资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: I was just about to say, this is a huge win for Republicans. Perhaps this is the most consequential decision by the court with campaign finance since Citizens United.
paragraphs.cn: 我正要这么说，这对共和党来说是一个巨大的胜利。这可能是自"公民联合"案以来，最高法院在竞选资金方面做出的最具影响力的裁决。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And we are going to have --
paragraphs.cn: 我们将 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Republicans are going to benefit.
paragraphs.cn: 共和党将受益。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- the conversation with that. I want to save time, because we have the head of the DCCC, meaning we have someone from the establishment whose job is to, like, raise that money.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 就此进行对话。我想节省时间，因为我们有民主党国会竞选委员会的主席，也就是说我们有一位来自建制派的人，其工作就是筹集这些资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: From the Democratic Party. Not the establishment.
paragraphs.cn: 来自民主党，不是建制派。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- and share, from the Democratic Party, also known as the establishment Democrats. Steve, thank you so much for explaining it.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 并且分享，来自民主党，也被称为建制派民主党人。史蒂夫，非常感谢你的解释。

paragraphs.speaker: Vladeck
paragraphs.text: Thanks for having me.
paragraphs.cn: 谢谢邀请。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You survived the group chat. You're going to get a mug. You can take that with you. All right. So today, we're going to talk about this ahead. President Trump testing out his new Qatari jet. And then LeBron James leaves the Lakers. Where is he going to go? Even politicians are trying to sway the star. And we're going to go to break with this view over the sunrise at Virginia beach.
paragraphs.cn: 你挺过了群聊。你会得到一个杯子。你可以带走。好了，今天我们要提前讨论这个。特朗普总统测试他的新卡塔尔飞机。然后勒布朗·詹姆斯离开湖人队。他会去哪里？甚至政客们也在试图影响这位球星。我们将以弗吉尼亚海滩日出的景色进入广告时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's now 28 minutes past the hour. This is your morning roundup. We want to go back to Venezuela and tell you what's going on with the search-and-rescue teams there. Because after those two deadly earthquakes, the death toll has climbed to more than 1,900 people. However, there is still reason for hope.
paragraphs.cn: 现在时间是整点过28分。这是你的早间新闻汇总。我们回到委内瑞拉，告诉你那里的搜救队情况。因为在那两次致命地震之后，死亡人数已攀升至1900多人。然而，仍有理由抱有希望。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: (SPEAKING FOREIGN LANGUAGE)
paragraphs.cn: （外语发言）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: New video showing rescue crews pulling a 3-year-old boy from the rubble of a collapsed building. He had been buried alive for six days. And today, a temporary Medicare program will begin coverage for weight loss medication. Millions will be able to receive GLP-1s for just $50 a month if they meet the right criteria. The program is expected to run through the end of 2027. And LeBron James leaving the L.A. Lakers. He does not plan to retire. He wants to be a free agent, according to sources.
paragraphs.cn: 新视频显示救援人员从倒塌建筑的废墟中救出一名3岁男孩。他被活埋了六天。今天，一项临时医疗保险计划将开始覆盖减肥药物。如果符合条件，数百万人每月只需支付50美元即可获得GLP-1类药物。该计划预计将持续到2027年底。勒布朗·詹姆斯离开洛杉矶湖人队。他不打算退役。据消息人士称，他想成为自由球员。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The rumors say he may return to Cleveland, but that's not confirmed. James's departure is leaving fans with mixed emotions.
paragraphs.cn: 有传言说他可能重返克利夫兰，但这尚未得到证实。詹姆斯的离开让球迷们百感交集。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I was hoping he'd stay in L.A., but the fact that he's going somewhere else is pretty cool. But LeBron is one of the greatest. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 我原本希望他留在洛杉矶，但他要去别的地方也挺酷的。但勒布朗是最伟大的球员之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Departure is leaving fans with mixed emotions.
paragraphs.cn: 离开让球迷们百感交集。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I was hoping he'd stay in L.A. But the fact that he's going somewhere else is pretty cool. But LeBron's one of the greatest.
paragraphs.cn: 我原本希望他留在洛杉矶。但他要去别的地方也挺酷的。但勒布朗是最伟大的球员之一。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I mean it was nice having him. And I think he should just retire.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，有他在很好。但我认为他应该退役。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I mean he won a championship. It was in a bubble, but he won. So, I guess -- I guess it would be all right. But in Lakers standards, it was -- it was a bust.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，他赢得了一个总冠军。虽然是在泡泡里，但他赢了。所以，我想 --  -- 我想这还不错。但按照湖人队的标准，这 --  -- 这是一个失败。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Almost every team in the NBA is sure to be circling. Even politicians getting in on it. Minnesota Governor Tim Walz posting this, trying to sway him to the Timberwolves. And straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, another traditional Democrat is out. Does the party have a bigger problem on its hands? The chair of the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, Congresswoman Suzan DelBene, joins the group chat next. And tonight, the U.S. faces Bosnia and Herzegovina for its first knockout game in the World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: 几乎每支NBA球队肯定都在关注。甚至政客也参与其中。明尼苏达州州长蒂姆·沃尔兹发帖试图说服他加盟森林狼队。接下来在CNN今晨节目中，又一位传统民主党人出局。该党是否面临更大的问题？民主党国会竞选委员会主席、众议员苏珊·德尔贝尼将加入群聊。今晚，美国队将在世界杯首场淘汰赛中对阵波黑队。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Whoever wins this match will make it to round 16. The U.S. is hoping to make it to their first quarterfinal since 2002.
paragraphs.cn: 无论谁赢得这场比赛，都将进入16强。美国希望自2002年以来首次进入四分之一决赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, the socialist wave alive and well in the Democratic Party. Last night's Colorado Democratic primary reveals 29- year-old Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeated 15-term incumbent Diana DeGette. Kiros is a lawyer, PhD student, who has not run for office before. She was born in 1997. That is the same year DeGette took office. MELAT KIROS (D),

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Congressional Candidate
paragraphs.text: Experience only matters if you actually do something with it. And we're finding now, with a lot of incumbent Democrats, that they're not actually able to deliver meaningfully on the things that working families need right now.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, whether traditional Democrats, as Antjuan says, wants to admit it or not, we're seeing a trend. In New York, Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three Democratic socialists in June primaries. They all won. In L.A., progressive city council member Nithya Raman forced a November runoff against incumbent Mayor Karen Bass. Here in Washington, D.C., Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George has advanced as the top contender for mayor.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And so, we have here Suzan DelBene, Democratic congresswoman, joining the chat because she chairs the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee. It is your job to get to 218, right, this fall? REP. SUZAN DELBENE (D-WA): Two hundred and eighteen in the House.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Two hundred and eighteen in the House. So, one of the reasons why I wanted to talk to you today, we talked earlier in the show about how different factions of Democrats were saying, we're going to spend. We're going to put behind people in various primaries. The Justice Democratic group was one of them. And they now have had at least seven wins. And those wins have been in California, Colorado, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania. I'm going to put aside philosophical differences.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: What does it mean when an outside group has had these kinds of gains and forced incumbents off -- out of the spot?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Well, let's be clear, the types of districts we're talking about, bright blue districts across the country, whereas the path to the majority is through purple districts across the country. That's really been our focus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, you're not worried about the general in those places?

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: The path to the majority, none of the --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Delbene
paragraphs.text: You know, what we've seen changes the math to get to the majority. We need to keep purple seats that we have and flip seats that Republicans have to get to 218. We have 71 districts on our map, 45 where we're on offense that we think we can flip. That's the math to get the majority. And frankly, in these districts, in these swing districts, we have incredible candidates running because they are connected to their communities and focused on really appealing across their communities.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，我们看到的这些情况改变了获得多数席位的计算方法。我们需要保住我们现有的紫色选区，并翻转共和党持有的席位，才能达到218席。我们的地图上有71个选区，其中45个是我们认为可以翻转的进攻目标。这就是获得多数席位的计算方法。坦率地说，在这些选区，在这些摇摆选区，我们有非常出色的候选人，因为他们与社区紧密相连，并专注于真正吸引整个社区的支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Delbene
paragraphs.text: These are communities with differing political views but it's about bringing people together.
paragraphs.cn: 这些社区有着不同的政治观点，但关键在于将人们团结在一起。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. So, are there any lessons to be drawn, though, from their successes? Are there any lessons to be drawn, even from, let's say, Colorado last night?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。那么，从他们的成功中能否吸取一些教训？甚至从昨晚的科罗拉多州，能否吸取一些教训？

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Well, there are absolutely lessons to be drawn, which is, have candidates who are deeply connected to their communities, focused on the issues that matter, and really are authentic, independent- minded voices for their communities, who are coming to Congress to get things done. We actually won last cycle, in 2024, 14 districts that Donald Trump also won. Fourteen Democrats won because we had strong candidates who spoke for people in their communities across ideological bounds.
paragraphs.cn: 当然可以吸取教训，那就是要有与社区紧密相连的候选人，专注于重要议题，真正为社区发出真实、独立的声音，他们来到国会是为了做成事情。实际上，在2024年的上一个选举周期，我们赢得了14个唐纳德·特朗普也曾赢得的选区。14名民主党人获胜，因为我们有强大的候选人，他们跨越意识形态界限，为所在社区的人民发声。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: And that, I think, is going to be critical to taking back the majority, including in Colorado.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，这对于夺回多数席位至关重要，包括在科罗拉多州。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me have you answered to some of the critics then. This is Dan Pfeiffer, co-host of "Pod Save America." I think people kind of tend to think of these guys as Obama bros. But here's what they're saying when they see this outside energy. DAN PFEIFFER, CO-HOST, "POD SAVE AMERICA": They have captured the energy. They are being more creative. They're being more strategic. They're being more aggressive.
paragraphs.cn: 那么让我听听你对一些批评者的回应。这是丹·法伊弗，《拯救美国播客》的联合主持人。我认为人们往往倾向于把这些家伙视为奥巴马的支持者。但这是他们看到这种外部能量时所说的话。丹·法伊弗，《拯救美国播客》联合主持人：他们抓住了这股能量。他们更有创意。他们更有策略。他们更具攻击性。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And if you are someone in the Democratic establishment, you should be deeply concerned about what this says about where the party is, what the standing of the party is, what the ability of the party infrastructure is to actually win elections.
paragraphs.cn: 如果你是民主党建制派的一员，你应该深切关注这说明了党的现状、党的地位以及党的基础设施实际赢得选举的能力。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can you respond to that? It's very specific.
paragraphs.cn: 你能回应一下吗？这很具体。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Well, I think it really comes down to having candidates that -- who I feel -- you know, communities feel like are speaking for them. And in these swing districts across the country, we have incredible candidates, farmers, veterans, mayors.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为这归根结底是要有候选人 --  -- 我觉得 --  -- 你知道，社区觉得他们在为自己发声。在全国各地的这些摇摆选区，我们有非常出色的候选人，有农民、退伍军人、市长。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He says you should be deeply concerned about what this says. And he talks about the ability of party infrastructure to actually win. He's not talking about what you're talking about, right? Like, do the locals connect? He's saying, can you guys help out your people compared to the outsiders who have captured some kind of energy?
paragraphs.cn: 他说你应该深切关注这说明了什么。他谈到了党的基础设施实际赢得选举的能力。他说的不是你说的那些，对吧？比如，当地人与社区的联系？他说的是，与那些抓住了某种能量的外部人士相比，你们能否帮助自己人？

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Absolutely. We gained seats last cycle, ran ahead of the top of the ticket, and are on a strong path across the country to pick up seats. Great candidates. I can't emphasize how important that is to have great candidates across the country, and they are running ahead of Republicans. They have -- they are incredible. I said, you know, farmers, mayors, veterans --
paragraphs.cn: 当然。我们在上一个周期赢得了席位，领先于总统候选人，并且在全国范围内都有强劲的势头来增加席位。优秀的候选人。我怎么强调都不为过，在全国范围内拥有优秀的候选人非常重要，而且他们领先于共和党人。他们 --  -- 他们非常出色。我说过，你知道，农民、市长、退伍军人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Including in Colorado, who also won in that primary last night in Colorado Five, Jessica Killin, a veteran, a mom, a strong voice for her community, going to flip a seat in Colorado Springs area. And Manny Rutinel, who won the primary in Colorado Eight, running against a Republican, Gabe Evans, who has been the example, kind of the poster child of what people are frustrated with Republicans, cutting health care, cutting food and nutrition.
paragraphs.cn: 包括在科罗拉多州，昨晚在科罗拉多第五选区的初选中获胜的杰西卡·基林，她是一位退伍军人、一位母亲、社区的有力代言人，将翻转科罗拉多斯普林斯地区的一个席位。还有曼尼·鲁蒂内尔，他在科罗拉多第八选区赢得了初选，将与共和党人加布·埃文斯竞争，后者一直是人们不满共和党人的典型代表，比如削减医疗保健、削减食品和营养。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So then let me talk money. Let me talk money. NRCC, right, your corollary, right now they have raised, at least according to CBS News, 164 million plus, DCCC, $139 million. When I look at the Supreme Court ruling yesterday, which says that there is going to be the ability to coordinate between candidates and party infrastructure on money, there are some people, Zolan help me out, what's the question here? Does it look good for Democrats given --
paragraphs.cn: 那么让我谈谈资金。让我谈谈资金。NRCC，对吧，你的对应机构，目前他们筹集了至少1.64亿美元，根据CBS新闻，而DCCC筹集了1.39亿美元。当我看到昨天最高法院的裁决，允许候选人和政党基础设施之间在资金上进行协调，有些人，佐兰帮我一下，问题是什么？这对民主党来说是否有利，考虑到 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: On -- when it comes to the war chest that they have? Some -- no. I mean you've got -- the Republicans have been able to amass massive donations, already signaling that after the Supreme Court decision you're going to see a flood towards something like the Ken Paxton race, right, as well as looking ahead to the general. I think of all those MAGA inc. dinners that have occurred in West Palm Beach as well. That's a little separate. But when it comes to that money difference right now, they do have an edge.
paragraphs.cn: 关于 --  -- 他们拥有的竞选资金？不。我的意思是，共和党人已经能够积累大量捐款，已经表明在最高法院裁决后，你会看到资金涌入像肯·帕克斯顿的竞选，以及展望大选。我想到了在西棕榈滩举行的所有那些MAGA Inc.晚宴。这有点不同。但就目前的资金差距而言，他们确实有优势。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I let her answer this because you're -- this is your job.
paragraphs.cn: 我能让她回答这个问题吗，因为这是你的工作。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Well, first of all, you talked about MAGA Inc. It turns out corruption helps folks raise money there. And I think that the reason Republicans wanted to see this change in campaign finance law is because they know that our candidates are stronger financially than their candidates.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，首先，你提到了MAGA Inc.。事实证明，腐败有助于他们筹集资金。我认为共和党人希望看到竞选财务法改变的原因是他们知道我们的候选人在财务上比他们的候选人更强。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But now that they have it, what do you do? I mean, Platner, right now, has seen his -- and he's running for Senate in Maine against Susan Collins, he has actually seen his lead diminish for a variety of reasons. But some of those reasons are being outspent according to their own campaign three to one in just the last two weeks.
paragraphs.cn: 但现在他们有了这个，你怎么办？我的意思是，普拉特纳，他现在正在缅因州竞选参议员，挑战苏珊·柯林斯，实际上他的领先优势因多种原因而缩小。但其中一些原因是在过去两周内，根据他们自己的竞选团队，被对手以三比一的支出超过。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: So, we don't need more. We need enough. We have incredible candidates. We are focused on the issues that matter. Families are struggling across the country with high costs. Republicans are indifferent. They don't care that the policies they put in place are hurting working families across the country. We are right on the issues. We have incredible candidates. We need to make sure we have the resources to get the message out, and we will. Republicans are focused on resource because they got nothing else out there.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们不需要更多。我们需要足够。我们有出色的候选人。我们专注于重要的问题。全国各地的家庭都在为高昂的成本而挣扎。共和党人漠不关心。他们不在乎他们实施的政策正在伤害全国各地的工薪家庭。我们在问题上是对的。我们有出色的候选人。我们需要确保有资源来传达信息，我们会的。共和党人专注于资源，因为他们没有其他东西了。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: People know that. We still are going to take back the majority all across the country, great candidates, and we'll have the resources to --
paragraphs.cn: 人们知道这一点。我们仍然会在全国范围内夺回多数席位，优秀的候选人，我们将有资源 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Well, one -- I want to let Antjuan jump in because his group chats are blowing up as we are sitting here.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。嗯，我想让安特万插话，因为他的群聊在我们坐在这里时已经炸开了。

paragraphs.speaker: Antjuan Seawright
paragraphs.text: It would not be uncommon for us to be outspent in any given election. Republicans have more millionaires and billionaires who --
paragraphs.cn: 在任何一场选举中，我们的支出被对手超过并不罕见。共和党人拥有更多的百万富翁和亿万富翁，他们 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Believe that no political romance without finance. That wouldn't be uncommon. Where our strength has always lied has been with people and the money -- momentum not necessarily money. And in this conversation we're having with -- this growing pains conversation we're having within our party with these primaries, look, I think growth is good.
paragraphs.cn: 相信没有金钱就没有政治浪漫。这并不罕见。我们的优势一直在于人民和资金 --  -- 势头不一定等同于金钱。在我们正在进行的这场对话中 --  -- 这场党内初选带来的成长阵痛对话 --  -- 我认为成长是好事。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: The real test for some of these candidates who do not consider themselves a part of the Democratic traditional infrastructure is going to be how they continue to galvanize or energize these voters for us to win up and down the ballot in November.
paragraphs.cn: 对于那些不认为自己是民主党传统基础设施一部分的候选人来说，真正的考验将是他们如何继续激励或动员这些选民，让我们在11月的选举中赢得从上到下的选票。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: We encourage and we --
paragraphs.cn: 我们鼓励并且我们 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But does that mean, Terry, for people like you, you're there writing, like spend here, force them to spend here, force them to spend here. Like, what is the counter?
paragraphs.cn: 但这是否意味着，特里，像你这样的人，你在那里写，比如在这里花钱，迫使他们在那里花钱，迫使他们在那里花钱。那么，对策是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.text: Look -- well, look, there's been a lot of talk about the infrastructure and the process problems that the Democratic Parties have. I think the real problem is actually policy. The Democratic Party, on economics, their messaging used to be so good, but then you had this horrible four years with Joe Biden where people saw 9.5 percent inflation.
paragraphs.cn: 听着 --  -- 嗯，听着，关于民主党的基础设施和程序问题已经有很多讨论。我认为真正的问题其实是政策。民主党在经济方面的信息传递曾经很好，但乔·拜登执政的这糟糕的四年里，人们看到了9.5%的通货膨胀。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Real quick. But at the same time, if Democrats could just moderate on men and women's sports, right?
paragraphs.cn: 说快一点。但与此同时，如果民主党人能在男女体育问题上采取温和立场，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: If they could moderate on this crazy trans stuff, you would make my life a lot harder and we wouldn't be able to run as hard a campaign ads. But they're too radical in the culture war.
paragraphs.cn: 如果他们能在这些疯狂的跨性别问题上采取温和立场，我的日子就会难过得多，我们就无法投放那么强硬的竞选广告。但他们在文化战争中太激进了。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: But, Terry, Terry --
paragraphs.cn: 但是，特里，特里 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: And every one of these socialists that's running, they're going to have to -- they're going to force all the people in the purple districts and swing districts to answer to them. James Talarico, is he really going to bring more Democrats across the finish line?
paragraphs.cn: 而且每一个参选的社会主义者，他们都将不得不 --  -- 他们将迫使所有紫色选区和摇摆选区的人回应他们。詹姆斯·塔拉里科真的能带领更多民主党人冲过终点线吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Terry. Terry, Terry, Terry, Terry --
paragraphs.cn: 特里。特里，特里，特里，特里 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me -- let me let Antjuan -- but, Antjuan, to his point, he's the guy who makes these kind of ads we're talking about. So --
paragraphs.cn: 让我 --  -- 让我让安特万 --  -- 但是，安特万，就他的观点而言，他是制作我们正在谈论的这种广告的人。所以 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Yes, they don't mind trying to distract us with these unnecessary issues. But --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，他们不介意用这些不必要的问题来分散我们的注意力。但是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: I don't think the culture is a distraction.
paragraphs.cn: 我不认为文化是一种干扰。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Let me finish. The bottom line is, the affordability crisis, brought to you and sponsored by Donald Trump --
paragraphs.cn: 让我说完。归根结底，由唐纳德·特朗普带来并赞助的可负担性危机 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: And Joe Biden before him.
paragraphs.cn: 以及在他之前的乔·拜登。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: Donald Trump, MAGA Mike, and the Republican majority is going to be your liability going into the midterms. Every single issue that you guys campaigned on in the 2024 election, you're upside down on, including the economy. You cannot run away from the fact that more Americans are paying more for gas, goods, groceries, services and health care, including the 18.5 million Americans in places that Donald Trump won who have seen their premiums, co-pays and deductibles rise because of your lack of action.
paragraphs.cn: 唐纳德·特朗普、MAGA迈克和共和党多数派将成为你们进入中期选举的负担。你们在2024年大选中竞选的每一个议题，你们都处于不利地位，包括经济。你们无法逃避这样一个事实：更多的美国人在汽油、商品、食品杂货、服务和医疗保健上支付更多，包括在唐纳德·特朗普赢得的地区的1850万美国人，由于你们的无所作为，他们的保费、共付额和免赔额都上涨了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I just want to bring it --
paragraphs.cn: 我只想提一下 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: And it's the one-year anniversary --
paragraphs.cn: 这是一周年纪念 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Of the big, ugly bill. Republicans across the board voting to slash healthcare, slash nutrition --
paragraphs.cn: 那个又大又丑的法案。共和党人全面投票削减医疗保健、削减营养 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: Basic economic issues.
paragraphs.cn: 基本的经济问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: You talk about economic issues.
paragraphs.cn: 你谈到了经济问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: So, the goal is to raise taxes on most Americans. That's what that bill primarily did.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，目标是对大多数美国人增税。那项法案主要就是做这个的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Ok, let me -- let me pause --
paragraphs.cn: 好的，让我 --  -- 让我暂停一下 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: No, go across the country. Go across --
paragraphs.cn: 不，走遍全国。走遍 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You guys, hold on one second. Let me pause. To your point, I understand and I hear what you're saying, but we are also going to have a segment about a frozen Congress where the president won't even let them sign their housing affordability bill in front of everyone. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: 各位，稍等一下。让我暂停一下。关于你的观点，我理解并听到了你的意思，但我们也会有一个关于国会冻结的环节，总统甚至不让他们当着所有人的面签署住房负担能力法案。（交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, so that affordability messaging is on a wobbly roller coaster.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，所以那个关于负担能力的信息就像在摇摇晃晃的过山车上。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: They know they're losing. They know they're losing.
paragraphs.cn: 他们知道自己正在输。他们知道自己正在输。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But for you guys, I think what I'm trying to understand is, do you end up pulling these folks closer, capturing that energy, or do things get bogged down in this battle about who's under what umbrella?
paragraphs.cn: 但对你们来说，我想我试图理解的是，你们最终是会把这些选民拉得更近，抓住那股能量，还是事情会陷入关于谁在哪个保护伞下的争斗中？

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: There is incredible energy around the candidates we have running in these purple districts across the country. I encourage you to go out -- go out to Colorado Five and see Jessica Killin and the work that she is doing to flip a seat there, and how much momentum she's built in her district. Or Manny Rutinel, who is the candidate in Colorado Eight. Go to Arizona and Joe Mendoza in Tucson area, who's also going to flip a seat in (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: 我们在全国这些紫色选区参选的候选人周围有着不可思议的能量。我鼓励你们去看看 --  -- 去科罗拉多第五选区，看看杰西卡·基林以及她为翻转那个席位所做的工作，以及她在选区积累了多少势头。或者曼尼·鲁蒂内尔，他是科罗拉多第八选区的候选人。去亚利桑那州图森地区的乔·门多萨那里，他也将翻转一个席位（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I hear the ads to come. But, yes.
paragraphs.cn: 我听到了即将到来的广告。但是，是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: They are -- these are folks who are incredible candidates and want to come and make a difference for their communities versus be a rubber stamp for Donald Trump.
paragraphs.cn: 他们 --  -- 这些人都是非常出色的候选人，他们想要来为他们的社区带来改变，而不是成为唐纳德·特朗普的橡皮图章。

paragraphs.speaker: Delbene
paragraphs.text: That's all Republicans are right now.
paragraphs.cn: 现在所有共和党人都是这样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Congresswoman, thank you so much for being here. It's not quite a rubber stamp. We're going to talk about that later, since you all are headed home for recess right now. But we do want to turn to this. Apparently, there are 250 presidential pardons for 250 years. Trump wants to have one of the largest clemency actions in modern history. And then, of course, it's a love story. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding celebration expected to take place at Madison Square Garden in New York City this week.
paragraphs.cn: 众议员女士，非常感谢您来到这里。这并不完全是橡皮图章。我们稍后会讨论这个问题，因为你们现在正要回家休会。但我们确实想转向这个话题。显然，250年来有250次总统赦免。特朗普想要采取现代历史上最大规模的宽大行动之一。然后，当然，还有一个爱情故事。泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔斯的婚礼庆祝活动预计本周在纽约市麦迪逊广场花园举行。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Sources telling CNN a rehearsal will happen tomorrow. Then the wedding is set for Friday. And then Tuesday CNN saw workers at MSG wearing a Taylor Swift carpenter's t-shirt. Was he just trolling? Is this confirmation? Either way, respect.
paragraphs.cn: 消息人士告诉CNN，明天将进行彩排。然后婚礼定在周五。周二，CNN看到麦迪逊广场花园的工作人员穿着泰勒·斯威夫特的木匠T恤。他只是在恶搞吗？这是确认吗？不管怎样，尊重。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: I have a lot of people have asked me for pardons. I call him Puff Daddy has asked me for a pardon. Well, I'm allowed to give her a pardon, but I -- nobody's approached me with it. Nobody's asked me about it.
paragraphs.cn: 很多人向我请求赦免。我称他为吹牛老爹的人请求我赦免。嗯，我可以给她赦免，但我 --  -- 没有人向我提出过。没有人问过我这件事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: President Trump has not been shy about using his executive power to grant pardons since returning to office. He may be getting ready to issue hundreds more. Now, according to a new report in "The Atlantic," the Trump administration is debating a plan to issue 250 pardons for America's 250th birthday. Who might be on the list? Well, joining us now in the group chat, Sarah Fitzpatrick, who's the author of that story.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统自重返办公室以来，毫不吝啬地使用行政权力进行赦免。他可能正准备再发布数百项赦免。现在，根据《大西洋月刊》的一篇新报道，特朗普政府正在讨论一项计划，为美国250岁生日发布250项赦免。谁可能出现在名单上？嗯，现在加入我们群聊的是莎拉·菲茨帕特里克，她是那篇报道的作者。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Sarah, can you give me some names? Who are they batting around? SARAH FITZPATRICK, STAFF WRITER, "THE ATLANTIC": So, there's quite a large name -- group of names that's being discussed, but there are some incredibly controversial names that have come up that even the lobbyists, even the people who are making these deals, feel very uncomfortable with. For example, the Malaysian fugitive Jho Low, who is credited with laundering billions and billions of dollars.
paragraphs.cn: 莎拉，你能告诉我一些名字吗？他们在考虑谁？《大西洋月刊》特约撰稿人莎拉·菲茨帕特里克：所以，正在讨论的名字很多，但出现了一些极具争议的名字，甚至连游说者、做这些交易的人都感到非常不舒服。例如，马来西亚逃犯刘特佐，他被认为洗了数十亿美元。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The rapper Pras, who was involved in an illegal influence campaign of Trump officials. And then we've seen even such a discussion, a very serious, high-level discussion of a pardon for the founder of the OneTaste orgasmic meditation cult. And so, we're seeing a kind of -- a mix of people who can afford to go after these pardons, as opposed to the traditional people that you would see pardons who truly are doing it on merit.
paragraphs.cn: 说唱歌手普拉斯，他参与了对特朗普官员的非法影响力活动。然后我们甚至看到了一场非常严肃、高层的讨论，关于赦免OneTaste orgasmic冥想邪教的创始人。所以，我们看到了一种 --  -- 混合了能够负担得起追求这些赦免的人，而不是你通常看到的那些真正凭功绩获得赦免的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: And I think that's what's making everyone very concerned. The other thing is there's -- on the list, I'm told, is quite a few foreign entities, people that are very rich, very well-connected from other countries. And so we're --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这就是让所有人非常担忧的原因。另一件事是，我被告知，名单上有相当多的外国实体，来自其他国家非常富有、人脉很广的人。所以我们 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Middle Eastern countries or --
paragraphs.cn: 中东国家，或者 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: A combination. Europe, Middle East, Asia. And so, I think that's giving everyone a lot of pause about, why are we considering, you know, this is an American -- a truly American clemency option. Why are we seeing these people on the list?
paragraphs.cn: 一个组合。欧洲、中东、亚洲。所以，我认为这让很多人停下来思考，为什么我们要考虑 --  -- 你知道，这是一个美国 --  -- 一个真正的美国赦免选项。为什么我们会看到这些人在名单上？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Right. Because if you're going to message it -- first of all, it's messaging at its base.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。因为如果你要传递信息 --  -- 首先，它本质上就是信息传递。

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: One hundred percent.
paragraphs.cn: 百分之百。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's 250th anniversary, 250 people. It's designed to be talked about. And so, you're saying there are people saying, if we want to talk about it, why would we pick kind of people who are seen as the worst of the worst, and also people who are not Americans or from other places.
paragraphs.cn: 这是250周年纪念，250个人。它就是为了引发讨论而设计的。所以，你说有人在说，如果我们想讨论这件事，为什么要选择那些被视为最坏的人，以及那些不是美国人或来自其他地方的人？

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: And also that our Justice Department, our law enforcement entities have spent so many years, hundreds and thousands of dollars prosecuting. You know, these are very, very serious cases. Of course, the president -- it's up to the president. The president can decide this at any moment. But it really does speak to kind of what is the purpose -- you know, is our justice system truly free and fair for all? Is everyone open to this or only those --
paragraphs.cn: 而且，我们的司法部、执法机构花费了这么多年，成百上千万美元进行起诉。你知道，这些都是非常非常严重的案件。当然，总统 --  -- 这取决于总统。总统可以随时决定。但这确实说明了目的 --  -- 你知道，我们的司法系统是否真正对所有人公平公正？是否每个人都享有这个机会，还是只有某些人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, I see Zolan nodding up a storm. I know the pardon thing within the White House presidential beat, people following it, has been -- people have been talking about this for a long time, ever since the sort of mass pardon that also included January 6th.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我看到Zolan在拼命点头。我知道白宫总统报道中的赦免问题，关注它的人 --  -- 人们已经讨论这个很久了，自从那次包括1月6日的大规模赦免以来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But it's been a long conversation about who gets a pardon and how that happens.
paragraphs.cn: 但关于谁获得赦免以及如何获得赦免，一直是一个漫长的讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: Who gets a pardon. That's the key, I think, here too, how that happens. It's worth reminding folks that there is a process in place for this. A process that --
paragraphs.cn: 谁获得赦免。我认为这也是关键，以及如何获得赦免。值得提醒大家的是，这有一个既定的程序。一个程序 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The process is not just calling him or his family?
paragraphs.cn: 这个程序不仅仅是给他或他的家人打电话？

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: No. No. Or not -- in a pre-Trump 2.0 sort of era, right, you have a situation where there's a long backlog for the clemency process, referrals made from experts, and then it's being processed through the Justice Department as well. The Office of the Pardon Attorney usually handling this as well. That has been thrown out of the door.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，或者说不是 --  -- 在特朗普2.0时代之前，对吧，你会遇到这样一种情况：赦免程序积压已久，专家提交了推荐意见，然后这些意见也在通过司法部处理。赦免检察官办公室通常也负责处理此事。但现在这一切都被抛之脑后了。

paragraphs.speaker: Kanno-Youngs
paragraphs.text: And here you have a system where there's almost a cottage industry that's been created, where lobbyists are jockeying with other allies of the president trying to win favor to get people pardoned.
paragraphs.cn: 而现在，这里几乎形成了一个家庭作坊式的体系，说客们与总统的其他盟友争相讨好，试图赢得赦免他人的机会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But, Sarah, can I ask, this is the kind of story where at some point someone will say, well, what about? What about Biden? What about Clinton? What about Obama? There's a lot of whataboutism when it comes to White House pardons. So, to you, what about that? Is this all that different or is this one of those Trump things where it's not different it's just out loud?
