paragraphs.cn: 是的。但是，莎拉，我能问一下吗，这种故事总会在某个时候有人说，那拜登呢？克林顿呢？奥巴马呢？在白宫赦免问题上，有很多"那又怎么说"的论调。那么，对你来说，这有什么不同？还是说这只是特朗普的又一件破事，其实没什么不同，只是被大声说出来而已？

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: I think what's different here is that there are people who are making money off of this in a -- at a mass --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为不同之处在于，有人正在大规模地从中牟利 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: People make money off of helping --
paragraphs.cn: 人们通过帮助他人牟利 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: And it's not just, you know, this is an example of, I think, a leadership vacuum or the mixed messaging that comes out of this White House. For example, this is a frenzy. I cannot tell you. I have lawyers and lobbyists who are telling me, I'm so exhausted I have to turn off my phone because everyone thinks that this is an option for them if they pay enough money.
paragraphs.cn: 而且，这不仅仅是，你知道，我认为这是领导力真空或白宫传递混乱信息的一个例子。例如，这简直是一场狂热。我无法告诉你。有律师和说客告诉我，他们累得不得不关掉手机，因为每个人都认为只要付足够的钱，这就是一个选择。

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: And the White House came out with a statement that was very interesting in response when we came to them with this reporting and said, the president, you know, thinks it's detestable that anyone would try and profit off this. But that's not the messaging that's coming from the president's closest aides. And they could, you know, in the Biden administration, we did see efforts to say, like, knock it off. Everything goes through DOJ. We are going to keep this very close and very tight. That's not the message here.
paragraphs.cn: 而白宫在我们报道此事后发表了一份非常有趣的声明，称总统认为任何人试图从中牟利都是可鄙的。但这并不是总统最亲密的助手们传递的信息。而且，你知道，在拜登政府时期，我们确实看到过努力说"别闹了"。一切都通过司法部处理。我们会把这件事控制得非常严密。这里传递的不是那个信息。

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: The message here is, when you come to the White House, hey, if you've got a client that might be interested, let us know. And so, I think that, you know, we're seeing the opportunities for real corruption happening at a massive scale.
paragraphs.cn: 这里传递的信息是，当你来到白宫，嘿，如果你有客户可能感兴趣，告诉我们。所以，我认为，你知道，我们正在看到大规模腐败的机会正在发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Right. And it only takes one of those names to come out that it goes sideways for it to blow back hard and backfire with the public. I want to turn to this because we're talking about this idea of pardons. Earlier, we were talking about what's happening in Congress. But what's not happening in Congress is legislating. Members of the House have gone home. They have no clear path for passing a list of very key bills.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。而且只需要其中一个名字曝光，事情就会变得糟糕，引发公众强烈反弹。我想转向这个话题，因为我们正在讨论赦免的想法。早些时候，我们讨论了国会正在发生的事情。但国会没有在做的是立法。众议院议员已经回家了。他们没有明确的途径来通过一系列非常关键的法案。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And here's the thing, hard line conservatives have refused to support some of these bills because they're frustrated with the president's lead issue, the SAVE America Act, his voting regulation act. President Trump has made this a priority, but there are divisions. Obviously, Senate Republicans say they don't have the votes to pass it. REP. HAKEEM JEFFRIES (D-NY): Mike Johnson and Steve Scalise have totally lost control of the House floor and are in the midst of a Republican civil war.
paragraphs.cn: 问题是，强硬派保守派拒绝支持其中一些法案，因为他们对总统的主要议题《拯救美国法案》 --  -- 他的投票监管法案 --  -- 感到失望。特朗普总统已将其列为优先事项，但存在分歧。显然，参议院共和党人表示他们没有足够的票数通过它。众议员哈基姆·杰弗里斯（纽约州民主党）：迈克·约翰逊和史蒂夫·斯卡利斯已经完全失去了对众议院议程的控制，正处于共和党内战之中。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Donald Trump is fighting with Senate Republicans. Senate Republicans are fighting with House Republicans. And House Republicans are fighting with each other. The entire thing is a mess. Meanwhile, House Democrats are continuing to fight to make life better for the American people.
paragraphs.cn: 唐纳德·特朗普正在与参议院共和党人斗争。参议院共和党人正在与众议院共和党人斗争。而众议院共和党人则在互相争斗。整个局面一团糟。与此同时，众议院民主党人继续为改善美国人民的生活而奋斗。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Shades on emoji. He's not wrong, right? They basically had to torpedo this vote because people are so upset about the SAVE Act not moving forward. They had to figure out a way to get it off the floor quickly. You're nodding. Have you been following this as well?
paragraphs.cn: 戴墨镜的表情符号。他说的没错，对吧？他们基本上不得不破坏这次投票，因为人们对《拯救法案》无法推进感到非常不满。他们必须想办法迅速将其从议程上撤下。你在点头。你也在关注这件事吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Fitzpatrick
paragraphs.text: I mean it's chaos. It's a level of chaos and it shows a level of kind of lack of message discipline in which, you know, we're not seeing actual -- Congress do its actual job, but people are really frustrated.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，这太混乱了。这种混乱程度表明缺乏信息纪律，我们看不到国会真正履行其职责，但人们确实感到非常沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But it's like the president wants this. I was reading a quote from Tim Burchett, a Republican, and he said, you know what? You guys are complaining about us being foils. We're in lock step with the White House, he said. So, is this a way of Trump, who ran into a brick wall in the Senate over lunch, finding a way to get the SAVE Act back?
paragraphs.cn: 但总统想要这样。我读到共和党人蒂姆·伯切特的一句话，他说，你知道吗？你们抱怨我们成为障碍。我们与白宫步调一致。那么，这是否是特朗普在午餐时在参议院碰壁后，找到让《拯救法案》重回正轨的一种方式？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: I think the Senate's the real problem here. I mean this -- we have -- we are so far away from the 1980s where --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为参议院才是真正的问题所在。我的意思是，我们离1980年代已经太远了，那时 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But is the answer is, he gets -- he gets a no there and so now it's like everyone in --
paragraphs.cn: 但答案是不是，他在那里遭到了拒绝，所以现在每个人都 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Well, what can he do?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，他能做什么呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Schilling
paragraphs.text: At this point we have the SAVE Act, which has five provisions that have 80 to 85 percent support amongst the American people. If you can't get that through the Senate, what can you get through? I don't know, spending trillions more dollars?
paragraphs.cn: 目前我们有《拯救法案》，其中五项条款得到了美国人民80%到85%的支持。如果连这个都无法在参议院通过，那还能通过什么？我不知道，再增加数万亿美元的支出？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, what you can't do then is talk about your affordability bill. You can't pass a defense bill. You can't do -- how -- like, there's so much stuff that you can't do because you're busy fighting for this particular thing.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，你就不能谈论你的负担能力法案。你不能通过国防法案。你不能做 --  -- 很多事你都不能做，因为你正忙于为这一特定事项而斗争。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: The least productive Congress in the history of this country. And the only thing --
paragraphs.cn: 这是美国历史上效率最低的国会。唯一的事情 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Thank you, Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: 谢谢，民主党人。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: The only thing we do in politics is make the comparison. You think about when Democrats were in charge, the most productive Congress we've had in generations, including bipartisan legislation that actually helped the American people.
paragraphs.cn: 我们在政治中唯一做的就是比较。想想民主党执政时，那是我们几代人以来最有成效的国会，包括真正帮助美国人民的跨党派立法。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.text: Productive (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: 有成效（听不清）。

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.text: That's why Donald Trump is going to be your biggest liability in November.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是为什么唐纳德·特朗普将在11月成为你们最大的负担。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Hey, like press, there's no such thing as bad production. OK, Terry, that name of the game is producing, and that's why we're ending with you because., my man, I hear you have news in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: 嘿，媒体朋友们，没有所谓的坏制作。好吧，特里，游戏的名字就是制作，所以我们以你结束，因为，老兄，我听说你的群聊里有新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
